description: This figure presents electron backscatter diffraction (EBSD) orientation imaging and statistical analysis of deformation-induced martensite (DIM) in a low-density steel (Fe-0.25C-3Mn-2Al-0.4Si) subjected to intercritical annealing at 750°C (AN750-T), 800°C (AN800-T), and 850°C (AN850-T) followed by low bainite aging at 400°C. Subfigures (a) and (b) display inverse pole figure (IPF) maps showing microstructural morphology before and after parent grain reconstruction, respectively, revealing multiphase microstructures consisting of ferrite, bainite/tempered martensite, fresh martensite, and retained austenite. Subfigure (c) presents the equivalent circle diameter distributions of DIM, demonstrating that the average DIM size increases with annealing temperature from 0.12 μm (AN750-T) to 0.15 μm (AN800-T) and 0.19 μm (AN850-T). The results indicate that higher annealing temperatures promote coarser microstructures and larger DIM sizes, correlating with reduced retained austenite content and altered strain partitioning behavior between soft ferrite and hard bainite/martensite domains.
